title: cardamine palustris subvar. sessiliflora
intro: Cardamine palustris subvar. sessiliflora, also known as marsh cress or bog cress, is a plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of compounds such as glucosinolates and isothiocyanates. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin problems like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
